steps.title: Use complementary angles
steps.content:
The angles $28^\circ$ and $62^\circ$ add to $90^\circ$, so they are complementary. Therefore,
$$\sin28^\circ=\cos62^\circ$$
and
$$\sin62^\circ=\cos28^\circ$$

steps.title: Substitute equivalent terms
steps.content:
Replace each sine value with the matching cosine value. This keeps the value the same while making it match one of the listed expressions.
$$(\sin28^\circ)(\cos62^\circ)+(\cos28^\circ)(\sin62^\circ)$$
$$(\cos62^\circ)(\cos62^\circ)+(\cos28^\circ)(\cos28^\circ)$$
$$(\cos62^\circ)^2+(\cos28^\circ)^2$$
The answer is C.
